activity.thought: I'm already here, I feel *good*, and my body's telling me it's not actually hungry yet—just full from earlier. My energy is solid and this bright, hopeful headspace won't last forever. I should use it now while I've got it, burn some of this restless energy, and maybe work through some of the rent stress that's lurking underneath.

I'm already here at the park and feeling genuinely good—sharp and hopeful. My body isn't hungry yet, and I've got solid energy to burn. This is exactly when exercise makes sense: I can use this momentum productively, stay healthy without spending money I can't spare, and maybe clear my head about the rent situation.

I'm genuinely full and my mind feels sharp right now—I can feel it. Skipping to the park makes perfect sense: I'll burn off this energy, stay healthy without draining my ⊙40, and maybe the movement will help untangle this rent stress that's creeping in. I can eat when my body actually asks for it.

I'm genuinely full right now—my body's telling me that clearly—and this good feeling is too valuable to waste. Exercise will use this energy productively, keep me healthy without spending money I don't have, and maybe clear my head about the rent stress that's lurking underneath. I can eat later when I actually need it.

I'm already full, so eating more right now won't actually help—my body doesn't need it. But I'm sharp and hopeful, and I should use this good headspace productively. Work would pay the bills that are crushing me, but the office isn't open yet. Exercise at the park sounds perfect: burn some energy, ride this momentum, and stay healthy without wasting money or time.

I'm in a really good headspace right now—sharp and hopeful—and my energy is solid. My stomach isn't screaming at me yet, but I did plan to eat, and skipping meals when I feel this good is usually a mistake. Plus I've got rent breathing down my neck at ⊙30 a day, so I should stick to the plan and fuel up before I lose this momentum.

blocks.note: I keep second-guessing myself, but my body has energy right now and the park will clear my head — I'm going before I talk myself out of it again.
After burning some of that energy I'll actually be hungry, and fueling up properly sets me up to work effectively.
